outline_json.title: The Jammed Gear

scenes.description: Mira climbs the tower stairs, noting the unusual quiet of the water wheel. She unlocks the gear room and finds the main gear jammed with a broken spoke lodged in the teeth.

mustEstablish.text: The main gear is jammed by a broken spoke lodged in the teeth.

mustTransferKnowledge.text: Mira discovers the gear is jammed.

scenes.description: Mira tries to free the gear with a pry bar but the spoke is wedged tight. She decides she must report this to the reeve immediately, despite the late hour.

mustTransferKnowledge.text: Mira learns the gear cannot be freed with a pry bar.

scenes.description: Mira hurries to the reeve's office and explains the jammed mechanism and the rising river. Kade demands proof of immediate danger and refuses to authorize a repair crew until morning.

mustEstablish.text: Reeve Kade refuses to authorize a repair crew until morning.

mustTransferKnowledge.text: Mira informs Kade of the jammed mechanism and rising river.
Mira hears Kade dismiss the urgency and demand proof.

scenes.description: Mira returns to the tower, frustrated but determined. She decides to inspect the river wall herself, fearing the worst. She finds Orin Pike already examining a fresh crack near the old mill.

mustEstablish.text: There is a fresh crack in the river wall near the old mill.

mustShowStateChange.text: Mira becomes frustrated but determined, deciding to ignore Kade's order and inspect the wall.

mustTransferKnowledge.text: Mira sees the fresh crack in the river wall.

scenes.description: Orin shows Mira the crack is widening and water is seeping through. They agree the wall could fail within hours. Mira must choose: obey Kade's order or act now to warn the town.

mustEstablish.text: The crack is widening and water is seeping through.
The wall could fail within hours.

mustShowStateChange.text: Orin's concern about the wall becomes apparent; he cooperates with Mira.

mustTransferKnowledge.text: Mira learns from Orin that the crack is widening.
Mira learns the wall could fail within hours.

outline_json.purpose: Mira discovers the bell mechanism is jammed during a routine inspection and reports it to Reeve Kade, who dismisses her urgency. That night, she and Orin Pike find a crack in the river wall, forcing Mira to choose between obeying Kade's order to wait until morning or taking action on her own.

establishedFacts.fact: The main gear is jammed by a broken spoke lodged in the teeth.
Reeve Kade refuses to authorize a repair crew until morning.
There is a fresh crack in the river wall near the old mill.
The crack is widening and water is seeping through.
The wall could fail within hours.

character_system_awareness.perspective: She knows it intimately and trusts it completely. She feels it is her duty to keep it running.
She respects the chain of command but resents when bureaucracy slows down emergency response.
He knows the bell tower's construction but not the mechanism. He trusts Mira's expertise.
He sees the council and reeve as necessary nuisances. He prefers to solve problems directly.
He knows the system exists and trusts its maintenance records. He does not understand the mechanics personally.
He knows the political systems intimately and uses them to maintain control. He believes in the chain of command.

customs_json: New masons must build a small wall or arch as a 'masterpiece' to qualify for the guild.
At the end of a job, the lead mason taps the final stone with a hammer three times for luck.
cultures.description: The guild responsible for building and maintaining the town's stone structures, especially the river wall. They are proud of their craft and pass down knowledge through apprenticeships. Orin Pike is a retired master mason.
cultures.name: Masons' Guild
cultures.speech_influences: Masons speak with precision, using technical terms for stone and mortar. They are often terse, but will explain things in detail if asked. They have a habit of measuring things with their hands or eyes before speaking.

system_views_json.sys_flood_warning: The bell is important, but the wall is what truly protects the town. They respect the bell keeper but see their own work as more fundamental.
system_views_json.sys_town_governance: The guild has a seat on the council and uses it to advocate for infrastructure funding. They resent the reeve if he delays repairs.

taboos_json: Using shoddy materials or shortcuts is a grave offense; a mason caught doing so may be expelled from the guild.
Taking credit for another's work is dishonorable.
values_json: Craftsmanship: doing a job well, even if no one sees it.
Tradition: using time-tested methods and materials.
Solidarity: masons look after each other and share knowledge.

customs_json: During flood season, every household in the lower ward keeps a packed bag ready for evacuation.
On the anniversary of the Great Flood, a silent procession walks the river wall at dusk.
When a new bell keeper is appointed, they must ring the bell once at noon on their first day, to prove the mechanism works (a loophole in the rule against ringing).
cultures.description: The general population of Brackenmarch, a mix of artisans, laborers, fishermen, and merchants. They are practical, hardworking, and deeply aware of the river's danger. They pride themselves on their self-reliance and their ability to work together in a crisis.
cultures.name: Townsfolk of Brackenmarch
cultures.speech_influences: Townsfolk speak directly and plainly, with a dialect that drops final consonants (e.g., 'goin' for 'going'). They use river metaphors ('high water' for trouble, 'low water' for peace). They avoid direct confrontation with authority but will grumble loudly among themselves.

system_views_json.sys_flood_warning: The bell is a sacred trust; its sound means immediate action. The bell keeper is respected but also held accountable.
system_views_json.sys_town_governance: The council is seen as distant but necessary; the reeve is respected for his authority but often criticized for being too cautious or too strict.

taboos_json: Speaking the word 'flood' during a storm is considered tempting fate.
Neglecting one's duty to the community (e.g., skipping a sandbag drill) is shameful.
Wasting resources, especially food and lamp oil, is seen as irresponsible.
values_json: Community solidarity: helping neighbors in need is a core virtue.
Practical skill: being good with one's hands is respected.
Caution: risk-taking is frowned upon, especially regarding the river.

facts.fact: Reeve Kade refuses to authorize a repair crew until morning.
The crack is widening and water is seeping through.
The main gear is jammed by a broken spoke lodged in the teeth.
The wall could fail within hours.
There is a fresh crack in the river wall near the old mill.

seed_json.premise: In a riverside canal town, the flood watch bell jams on the same night the river wall cracks. A careful bell keeper, a retired mason, and a strict town reeve must reach the old tower, repair the gear, and sound the warning before the lower ward floods.

characters.name: Mira Vale
characters.description: Bell keeper, early 30s. Precise, practical, and trusted to maintain the flood watch tower. She notices small mechanical changes quickly and feels responsible for every family in the lower ward.

characters.name: Orin Pike
characters.description: Retired mason, late 60s. Helped build the old river wall and knows its weak points. Gruff but steady, he carries tools everywhere and trusts evidence more than authority.

characters.name: Tomas Kade
characters.description: Town reeve, mid-40s. Responsible for avoiding panic during storms. He is not malicious, but his need for order makes him resist Mira's warning until the evidence is undeniable.

constraints_json: The mechanism cannot be repaired from outside; the gear room must be accessed.
If the water wheel is damaged or the channel is blocked, the system has no backup power.
The bell cannot be rung manually from the ground; only the mechanism can sound it.
world_systems.description: A water-wheel-driven mechanism that strikes a large bronze bell atop the flood watch tower. The water wheel is engaged by a lever in the gear room; when engaged, a series of gears and cams rotate a hammer that strikes the bell at regular intervals. The system is designed to be simple and robust, but requires regular maintenance.
manifestations_json: The bell is heard throughout the town; its tone is deep and carries over the sound of the river.
During maintenance, Mira tests the mechanism by hand-cranking it, but the bell is not rung—only the striker is moved to ensure it's free.
world_systems.name: Flood Warning System
rules_json: The bell can only ring if the water wheel is engaged and the gear train is free of obstructions.
The water wheel must have sufficient water flow to turn; in low water, the bell may not sound as loudly or at all.
The mechanism can be stopped by disengaging the lever, which locks the gear train.
vocabulary_json: Striker: the hammer that hits the bell
Cam: the shaped wheel that lifts the striker
Engagement lever: the lever that connects the water wheel to the gear train

constraints_json: The reeve cannot raise taxes or declare martial law without the baron's permission.
The council cannot remove the reeve, but they can appeal to the baron.
Emergency funds are limited; the town relies on a reserve that has been depleted by recent poor harvests.
world_systems.description: Brackenmarch is governed by a town council of guild masters, who elect a mayor from among themselves. The baron's appointed reeve has authority over public safety, infrastructure, and law enforcement. The reeve can veto council decisions on safety matters, but the council controls the purse strings. This creates a delicate balance of power.
manifestations_json: Town meetings are held in the town hall, where guild masters argue over budgets.
The reeve's office issues permits and collects fines.
Disputes between the reeve and council are common, often mediated by the mayor.
world_systems.name: Town Governance
rules_json: The reeve can order evacuations and emergency measures without council approval during a crisis.
The council must approve all non-emergency spending, including repairs to the river wall.
The bell keeper is appointed by the council and cannot be dismissed by the reeve alone.
vocabulary_json: Guild master: head of a craft guild
Mayor: elected head of the council
Town council: body of guild masters
